excerpt: Bank of Ireland Sustainability Report 2023 [...] Decarbonising our own Operations In 2023, we demonstrated our commitment to GHG reduction within our own operations [...] by achieving over 85% of our SBTi 2030 target reduction in GHG emissions from our own operations (Scope 1 and 2). [...] Scope 1 GHG emissions Scope 1 covers CO2e emissions from heating using oil, natural gas, refrigerants and from the usage of the Group's controlled company cars. [...] Scope 2 GHG emissions Scope 2 covers emissions from electricity [...] Scope 3 GHG emissions Scope 3 covers CO2e emissions from: Business travel (air, road and rail) and downstream leased assets [...] Financed emissions The Group will commence reporting of financed emissions from H2 2024 in line with Pillar 3 ESG Reporting timelines. [...] Managing Climate-related Risks In the effort to mitigate the most severe consequences of climate change, there is a global push to reduce GHG emissions and limit the rise in global average temperatures to below 2°C, with the aim of mitigating the increase to 1.5°C. Businesses and communities will continue to be forced to adapt to physical changes as the world transitions to a low carbon economy, resulting in risks and opportunities in the short, medium and long-term. Our strategy execution is informed by our assessment of risks and opportunities. [...] The key climate-related risk impacts are: Credit risk: Increased costs associated with physical and transition risks may impact financial soundness of households and businesses reducing their ability to service debt and impairing asset values, resulting in financial loss to the Group through higher probability of default and higher losses given default. Business and Strategic risk: Long term franchise impacts if strategic commitments are not achieved by the Group and the Group's product offering does not adapt to changing market dynamics. Operational risk: Physical risks could impact continuity of the Group's operations or operations of its material suppliers, resulting in sustained disruption of the supply chain and ultimately our ability to service customers. [...] In the Group's key planning process, the Internal Capital Adequacy Assessment Process (ICAAP), the potential impact of transition and physical risk drivers is assessed for each key risk type [...] Furthermore, in line with the ECB's guidelines on climate-related and environmental risks and the recommendations of the TCFD, the Group defines two key sub-categories of climate-related risks [...] First Line of Defence (1LOD): The business divisions or functions taking on risk have the primary responsibility for managing the risk generated by their actions and this includes managing ESG factors. Second Line of Defence (2LOD): has the responsibility for ensuring the ESG risk factors are considered when executing second line responsibilities as set out in the Group Risk Management Framework. [...] Third Line of Defence (3LOD): is responsible for ensuring the first and second lines of defence assess whether all significant risks are identified and appropriately reported by management to the executive and board of management [...] Regulatory risk: Failure to implement in a timely manner ongoing changes in climate regulation could affect the Bank's profitability through regulatory sanctions.

source_location.file: Bank of Ireland Group plc BOI-Sustainablilty-Report-2023.jsonl
source_location.subsection: Decarbonising our own Operations / Managing Climate-related Risks

ER: ECB's guidelines on climate-related and environmental risks
GHG: transitions to a low carbon economy
MBV: probability of default and higher losses given default

edges_claimed.evidence: Furthermore, in line with the ECB's guidelines on climate-related and environmental risks and the recommendations of the TCFD, the Group defines two key sub-categories of climate-related risks [...] Second Line of Defence (2LOD) has the responsibility for ensuring the ESG risk factors are considered when executing second line responsibilities as set out in the Group Risk Management Framework. [...] Climate risk scenario analysis for Credit Risk integrated into the Group ICAAP. [Three-lines-of-defence governance drives TCFD/ECB-aligned disclosure and ICAAP climate-risk reporting.]

In 2023, we demonstrated our commitment to GHG reduction within our own operations by achieving over 85% of our SBTi 2030 target reduction in GHG emissions from our own operations (Scope 1 and 2). [...] The Group will commence reporting of financed emissions from H2 2024 in line with Pillar 3 ESG Reporting timelines. [SBTi-aligned disclosure + Pillar 3 ESG financed-emissions reporting drive the operational GHG reduction achieved in 2023.]

Credit risk: Increased costs associated with physical and transition risks may impact financial soundness of households and businesses reducing their ability to service debt and impairing asset values, resulting in financial loss to the Group through higher probability of default and higher losses given default. Business and Strategic risk: Long term franchise impacts if strategic commitments are not achieved by the Group [...] Regulatory risk: Failure to implement in a timely manner ongoing changes in climate regulation could affect the Bank's profitability through regulatory sanctions. [Unmitigated GHG/climate risk propagates through credit, strategic and regulatory risk into financial loss, impaired asset values, franchise damage, and lower Bank profitability — i.e. GHG exposure reduces firm value.]

causal_markers: embedding climate-related impacts in key decision making processes
notes: Relaxed-wording full Chain A preserved across pp.29-34 (ESRS2) in 'Decarbonising our own Operations' and 'Managing Climate-related Risks'. The chain reads: the Group's three-lines-of-defence risk governance, Group Risk Management Framework and board of management (CG, L45, L50) drive alignment with TCFD / ECB guidelines / SBTi / Pillar 3 ESG Reporting / ICAAP climate-risk scenario analysis (ER, L41, L43, L45, L47); SBTi-aligned disclosure and financed-emissions reporting deliver the 85% Scope 1+2 GHG reduction achievement and the low-carbon-economy transition (GHG, L41, L43); and unmitigated climate/GHG risk propagates through credit, strategic and regulatory risk channels into financial loss, impaired asset values, franchise damage and reduced Bank profitability (MBV, L43, L53). Wording tolerances relaxed vs. strict Chain A spec: MBV is anchored on 'financial soundness / financial loss to the Group / Bank's profitability / long term franchise impacts' rather than explicit 'shareholder value / market capitalisation'; ER is anchored on SBTi + TCFD + ECB guidelines + ICAAP + Pillar 3; CG is anchored on the three-lines-of-defence risk governance and the Group Risk Management Framework. Edge signs match Chain A hypothesis (+, -, -). Causal markers are dense across the passage, with an explicit forward narrative from governance to disclosure, disclosure to emissions, and emissions to firm value.
